programmes.show_description: Grapes of Wrath: To avoid an oddly sensual cleaner, Bernard and Manny accept a house-sitting job, where they drink a bottle of wine that's much more expensive than they realise.
The Blackout: Feeling hyper after drinking too many espressos and watching an entire box-set of The Sweeney, Manny chases a bag snatcher and is mistaken for a policeman.
A highlights compilation from 2011. Jimmy Carr oversees the disputes and deliberations with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and an array of their celebrity guests.
A show recorded to mark the Queen's Diamond Jubilee. Jimmy Carr hosts, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, plus guests Sarah Millican, Miles Jupp, Chris Kamara and Jennie Bond.
Grapes of Wrath: To avoid an oddly sensual cleaner, Bernard and Manny accept a house-sitting job, where they drink a bottle of wine that's much more expensive than they realise.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
The One at a Welsh Theme Park: Thrill-seeking Tom wants to give the love of his life Becca the ride of her life, as he plans their wedding at his favourite theme park.
The One in an Aquarium: Essex groom Gary plans an aquatic wedding 200 miles from home. But Lauren hopes for a home counties service. Will Gary's underwater dream sink without a trace?
The Oktoberfest One: Sofia dreams of a boho beach blessing, but Craig wants an Oktoberfest wedding with short leather trousers. Will Sofia end the nuptials?
Stephen J Cloobeck, CEO of hotel firm Diamond Resorts International, is the first boss to go undercover in his business for a second time, as he visits Pacific Monarch Resorts.
Steve Greenbaum, co-founder and CEO of PostNet, which helps small businesses with everything from printing to design, goes undercover to see how well his firm is functioning.
In Warwickshire, there's funny facial hair, soppy pet names and a saucy discovery, as three rival couples put their hosting skills to the test.
In Coventry, joker Neal and Hazel plan a 'restaurant-quality' menu. A shock discovery on Jess and Ed's night has the group in hysterics. And Emma and Huseyin serve up Turkish delights.
In Berkshire, Julian and Lesley showcase Julian's home-cured pastrami, Tash and Rob's 'healthy' food fails on presentation, and Chris and Amanda's love theme brings laughs - and some tears.
In Reading, classy Liz and David recreate their first ever dinner party. Models Franky and Charli discover a secret admirer. And can jokers Jo and Steve take their own night seriously?
Curtis House Inn: Gordon visits Connecticut's oldest inn, the Curtis House. Run by siblings who no longer talk to each other, things are falling apart. Will their legacy be lost?
Historic Calumet Inn: Gordon visits the struggling Historic Calumet Inn in Pipestone, Minnesota, where the overworked staff are on the brink of revolt.
Angler's Lodge: Gordon Ramsay visits Angler's Lodge in Idaho, a stunning venue whose owners have been struck by family tragedy. Can he rescue the broken business?
Vienna Inn: Gordon checks in to the Vienna Inn, an Austrian-themed hotel and restaurant in Massachusetts, where business isn't what it used to be. Can he keep their doors open?
Town's Inn (Part 1/2): Gordon's at Harpers Ferry in West Virginia to visit The Town's Inn, a historic hotel that's so full of clutter it can barely accommodate guests.
It's Joe Wilkinson and Josh Widdicombe v Rhod Gilbert and Thaniya Moore. Frankie Monroe joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Plus, of course, Rachel and Jimmy too.
Filmed at Wembley Arena at the end of his sell-out 2007 UK tour, the one-man comedy show includes Bill's trademark musical inventiveness and verbal brilliance.
The Big Lock-out: The shop has a new security system that neither Bernard nor Manny can operate, resulting in Bernard being locked out for the evening, with Manny locked inside.
He's Leaving Home: Manny runs away when Bernard's bossiness gets too much. But life on the street is tough, so when a photographer offers him a place to stay, Manny moves in...
Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show. Rob Beckett, Paisley Billings and Josh Jones pit their wits against Katherine Ryan, Tom Read Wilson and Kiri Pritchard-McLean.
Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson play the panel game with guests Reginald D Hunter, Rachel Riley, Josh Widdicombe and Gemma Collins.
With host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and their guests Sarah Millican, Micky Flanagan, Georgie Thompson and Louie Spence.
The Big Lock-out: The shop has a new security system that neither Bernard nor Manny can operate, resulting in Bernard being locked out for the evening, with Manny locked inside.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Lorne Abony, the CEO and chairman of in-store media provider Mood Media, one of the fastest-growing companies in America, adopts a disguise to go undercover in his own business.
Jim Rogers, the chairman and CEO of Kampgrounds of America, the largest campsite organisation in the world, goes undercover in his business, disguised as an accountant.
In the Lake District, Paul and Glenda welcome their rival diners to their caravan. Gadget geek David's hosting leaves a lot to be desired. Matt and Polly serve home-grown 'good life' grub.
In Kent, Stephanie and Ian's home, a tiny version of the Palace of Versailles, amazes their guests. Is their food as impressive? Later in the week, there's dressing up - and stripping off.
...Selling: The brothers take charge when single mum Lolita's home needs overhauling. Working with a tight budget in a red-hot real estate market, can she take the heat?
The Northern Soul One: Nathan loves music and Stoke-on-Trent, so when bride-to-be Beccie gives him free rein to organise their wedding, he plans Northern Soul nuptials.
The One on a Thai Farm: After a hilltop proposal, hopeless romantic Gavin plans nuptials in Somerset for his fiancée Sophie, with a combined theme of farmyard and Thai.
Anthony Wedo, the president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover among his workforce. Can he turn the firm around after years of challenges?
Amit Kleinberger, CEO of frozen yogurt chain Menchie's, goes undercover to work out what changes must be made at grassroots level to make the company a billion-dollar business.
Applegate River Lodge: In Oregon, Gordon Ramsay tries to rescue the Applegate River Lodge, which is around $1 million in debt. Can he help the owners turn their fortunes around?
Hotel Chester: Gordon visits Mississippi to rescue the failing Hotel Chester. The owner has 40 years' experience in the hotel industry but is struggling to make things work.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Francis and Juanita love their home but they've outgrown it. Can Jonathan make it look brand new while Drew searches for a high-class house?
Loose Lips Sink Trysts: After kissing Danni, Gary tries to correct course - but Daisy finds out anyway. And Diana indulges in a forbidden secret.
The five comics go undercover as they try to impress Taskmaster Greg Davies with their attempts at high-level espionage. And Ed Gamble shaves his knees.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges take on Joe Wilkinson and Victoria Coren Mitchell. Adam Kay joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Roisin Conaty take on guest captain Alan Carr and Joe Lycett. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
The Au Pair: The family's cream-tea-obsessed former au pair pays the Goodmans a surprise visit. But there's nothing sweet about this reunion.
Dad's Birthday: Dad has the birthday blues - he's worked out the date when he's going to die, and 'Horrible Grandma' turns up uninvited. Can Jim's magic do the trick?
